name: sprint-cli-frames-draw
description: Variant of the canonical sprint pipeline for adding frame + drawing-shape CREATION to the EnsembleWorks CLI (a server/contracts slice, unlike the frame-picker client/chrome slice it descends from). Same shape as sprint-frame-picker: skips persona-interview + dogfood, swaps executable acceptance tests for an agent-driven CLI->browser walkthrough (repo has no acceptance harness), keeps the multi-model code-quality gate before the human bossman gate. DIFFERENCE vs sprint-frame-picker: design-protos (ux-prototype) is swapped for an api-surface design note (there is no UI to prototype; the risky design is the exact tldraw-5.1.0 record shapes), and that note gets its OWN paired adversarial review gate (api-surface-review) per the Second Iron Rule.

inputs.goal: Let a CLI-only agent create frames and drawing shapes, reparent shapes into/out of frames, and delete a frame without corrupting its contents. New CLI surface: canvas shape create frame|line|draw|highlight; canvas shape update <id> --frame|--to-page|--rotate|--lock (reparent + riders); canvas shape delete <id> [--with-children]. Contracts + server only; the CLI is a pure manifest projection (zero cli/src changes).

steps.description: Enumerate acceptance criteria as a CHECKLIST (this repo has no executable acceptance harness — do not fabricate one). Cover each new verb (frame/line/draw/highlight create, reparent in/out, rotate/lock, delete default vs --with-children), that each record RENDERS in the browser, that reparent does not visually jump the shape, and the excluded scope stays unbuilt.

gate.prompt: Approve the AC checklist. Is every new verb + delete/reparent semantic specified end-to-end and objectively checkable, with the browser-render check explicit?

retro.lesson: When a repo doesn't do executable acceptance tests our normal way, enumerate ACs as a checklist and verify with an agent-driven walkthrough of the live app — match the repo's conventions, don't import ceremony.

steps.description: Mechanical pre-build gates: the new unit tests fail correctly (RED-is-real — assertion on the real expected record, not a missing module) and every checklist item is objectively checkable.

SWAP for design-protos (no UI to prototype). Author the API-surface design note: exact verb signatures + flags, and the EXACT tldraw-5.1.0 record props for each new type (frame needs color + non-zero w/h; line points is a keyed {id,index,x,y} dict; draw/highlight segments are base64 via compressLegacySegments + scaleX/scaleY; highlight has no fill/dash/isClosed), plus every error case (bad/missing points -> 400, frame not found -> 404). The record-shape precision IS the risky design here.

NEW paired review (Second Iron Rule: the design note is an artifact and must be refuted before it is trusted). Fresh-context adversarial claim-verification: for EVERY record prop the note specifies, confirm it against the real @tldraw/tlschema 5.1.0 validator and the server put-validation path (mark each VERIFIED / FALSE / UNVALIDATED with a file:line). Attack: would any record 400 on put? are required props missing? is the base64 segment / keyed-line-points shape exactly right? Also flag over-engineering and any excluded-scope leakage.

gate.prompt: Every record-prop claim is VERIFIED against tldraw 5.1.0; no record would 400 on put; no excluded scope crept in.

steps.description: Plan the slice against the reuse map (shape.ts base-record builder + getIndexAbove/sortByIndex, canvas/geometry.ts pagePoint, canvas/frames-helper.ts findFrameByName, @tldraw/tlschema compressLegacySegments, scribe-api.test.ts real-app test pattern). Order: pure drawShapes helper -> contracts enum/fields -> server branches.

Adversarial fresh-context review of the plan: refute every file:line reuse claim against the real code; re-verify every record-prop claim against tldraw 5.1.0; flag over-engineering vs the locked scope and any excluded-scope (eraser/laser/align/group/image-upload) leakage.

TDD: Navigator writes the failing node:assert tests (pure drawShapes helpers first, then HTTP round-trips that boot the real app), Driver implements: drawShapes.ts helper -> contracts enum/fields + read-output extension -> server create branches + update-reparent + delete-children + the frame read-symmetry extension (frames.ts surfaces geo/line/draw/highlight). SCOPE NOTE: the AC-gate human review added read symmetry (AC15-AC17) and hardened every geometry AC to a numeric decoded-record assertion. Match repo conventions exactly (node:assert under src/, boot real app in-process, real SQLite, no mock lib, no new deps).
Test-pyramid + no-mock + test-first-commit checks; typecheck + build green across all workspaces (build also refreshes the CLI embedded manifest snapshot).

Agent-driven acceptance: bin/dev up, then drive the CLI (ew canvas shape create frame|line|draw|highlight, reparent a sticky in/out, delete a frame both ways) against a live room, and verify IN THE BROWSER that each record renders (frame clips its children; line/draw/highlight visible) and both delete paths behave. Record PASS/FAIL + screenshot/log evidence per checklist line. The CLI-writes -> browser-renders loop is the real end-to-end.

Multi-model quality gate: three independent judges on DIFFERENT model families (claude-opus-4-8, gemini, codex) each score the diff + tests + neighbor files against a repo-derived rubric and answer 'at least as high quality as the surrounding code?'. Rubric: naming/altitude match neighbors (shape.ts, canvas/*); tests indistinguishable from scribe-api.test.ts (node:assert, boots real createSyncApp, real SQLite, no mock lib, pure logic extracted to drawShapes.ts + duck-typed/real records); no new deps (compressLegacySegments already in @tldraw/tlschema); record props EXACTLY match tldraw 5.1.0 (no 400 on put); typecheck+build green; RED-is-real; nothing built for the excluded scope.

gate.prompt: All responding judges return at_least_as_good=true with zero must-fix findings (each must-fix verified on the merits against the real code before it is accepted or refuted).

retro.lesson: Have multiple models independently confirm new code + tests are at least as good as the surrounding code before a friend's-repo PR. Judges are adversarial finders, not vetoes — check each must-fix against the real code before accepting or refuting it.

steps.description: Human gate: browser walkthrough with the user of the built verbs against the AC checklist (CLI writes -> browser renders). THE AUTONOMOUS RUN STOPS HERE and waits for the human.
Retrospect the slice; append learnings to the ledger.
Open the PR against main with unit tests, the completed AC checklist + walkthrough evidence, and the quality-panel verdict attached. No version bump / tag / merge (maintainer-cut only).
